messages.content:
This is a fast first-pass software engineering task for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one compact JSON object. Do not include markdown or prose outside JSON.
Shape: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"tabIds":[1],"reason":"Short reason."}],"reviewTabIds":[2]}
Use tabIds arrays, not full tab objects.
Use at most 24 broad semantic buckets.
Allowed colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Prefer broad, useful semantic topics over domain-only grouping.
This is a coarse pass: mixed or large buckets are acceptable because a second pass will refine them.
Every eligible tab id must appear exactly once, either in buckets[].tabIds or reviewTabIds.
Put generic, sensitive, or very uncertain tabs in reviewTabIds.
Use sequenceIndex and index as ordering signals. Adjacent tabs often belong together.
Do not create a broad catch-all bucket for unrelated leftovers; use reviewTabIds instead.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.

message.content: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"chrome_extension_dev","title":"Chrome Extension Development","color":"blue","confidence":0.94,"tabIds":[10001,10018,10028,10038,10045],"reason":"Chrome extension APIs and side panel or tab-group implementation docs."},{"bucketKey":"frontend_ui_impl","title":"Frontend Implementation","color":"green","confidence":0.9,"tabIds":[10004,10014,10021,10031,10041],"reason":"CSS, React state, and Playwright work for building the UI."},{"bucketKey":"ui_design_patterns","title":"Product UI Design","color":"purple","confidence":0.89,"tabIds":[10010,10012,10020,10039,10047],"reason":"Design references for side panels, switches, and layout patterns."},{"bucketKey":"cloudflare_gateway","title":"Cloudflare Worker Gateway","color":"cyan","confidence":0.92,"tabIds":[10003,10013,10030,10040,10011],"reason":"Worker routing, tunnels, and related deployment or security setup."},{"bucketKey":"database_sql","title":"Database and SQL Work","color":"yellow","confidence":0.87,"tabIds":[10005,10022,10024,10032],"reason":"Database notes, migrations, and query optimization tasks."},{"bucketKey":"ai_coding_agents","title":"AI Coding Agents","color":"red","confidence":0.9,"tabIds":[10000,10008,10027,10035,10037],"reason":"Coding-agent tools, orchestration, benchmarks, and MCP integration docs."},{"bucketKey":"llm_eval_research","title":"LLM Evaluation Research","color":"pink","confidence":0.94,"tabIds":[10002,10009,10019,10029,10046],"reason":"Leaderboards, papers, datasets, and benchmark research for LLM evaluation."},{"bucketKey":"video_media_queue","title":"Video and Media Queue","color":"grey","confidence":0.85,"tabIds":[10006,10016,10026,10033,10043],"reason":"Videos and talk transcripts queued for later watching or review."},{"bucketKey":"reading_queue","title":"Read Later","color":"grey","confidence":0.82,"tabIds":[10015,10023,10025,10042],"reason":"Background reading and long-form reference material."},{"bucketKey":"shopping_billing","title":"Shopping and Billing","color":"yellow","confidence":0.72,"tabIds":[10007,10017,10034,10036,10044],"reason":"Purchase, pricing, invoice, and finance-related comparison tabs."}],"reviewTabIds":[]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Considering JSON formatting**

I need to ensure that I create a compact JSON format, probably defaulting to Chinese, but I'll stick with English for titles since that's dominant. Every tab should appear exactly once, covering broad semantic buckets. I'm also thinking about reviewing categories for shopping and finance, as well as considering separate sections for read-later or video. Let’s aim to craft something with 24 or fewer categories overall. It’s all about clarity and efficiency!

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}]}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Classify tabs by semantic topic, task, or intent. Prefer useful cross-domain groups over domain-only grouping.
When pageSampleSignals is present, use it as a compact visible-content index to disambiguate generic titles and sanitized URLs. Keep grouping fine-grained; do not merge broad workflows just because sampled pages are all readable.
Do not close, discard, navigate, or execute tabs. You only produce grouping intent.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create large generic catch-all groups. Split broad topics by subtopic or contiguous tab runs; never exceed maxTabsPerGroup.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: medium, balance quality and speed.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.
Refine this coarse bucket: Shopping and Billing.
Coarse reason: Purchase, pricing, invoice, and finance-related comparison tabs.
This is a large-session refinement pass; prefer concise semantic clustering and strict JSON over deep reasoning.
Split it only when there are clearly different semantic tasks or topics.
Keep uncertain or sensitive tabs in reviewTabs.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"purchase_comparison","title":"Purchase comparison","color":"green","confidence":0.92,"ids":[10007,10034],"reason":"Amazon checkout/comparison tabs aligned by purchase evaluation intent across the session."},{"key":"pricing_review","title":"Pricing review","color":"blue","confidence":0.9,"ids":[10017,10044],"reason":"Bank pricing pages focused on subscription or pricing comparison rather than checkout or invoicing."},{"key":"invoice_billing_portal","title":"Invoice and billing portal","color":"yellow","confidence":0.95,"ids":[10036],"reason":"Stripe invoice portal is a distinct billing-management task."}],"review":[]}
message.reasoning_content:
**Classifying tasks and tabs**

I need to output one JSON object that classifies all eligible tabs exactly once. It looks like the dominant language is likely English. I want to refine my categorization related to Shopping and Billing. For the tabs, I’ll clearly split them into different tasks or topics. Possible groups include Purchase comparison for the Amazon tabs, Pricing review for the bank tabs, and the Invoice/billing portal. My confidence in this classification is decent.

messages.content:
This is a fast first-pass software engineering task for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one compact JSON object. Do not include markdown or prose outside JSON.
Shape: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"tabIds":[1],"reason":"Short reason."}],"reviewTabIds":[2]}
Use tabIds arrays, not full tab objects.
Use at most 24 broad semantic buckets.
Allowed colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Prefer broad, useful semantic topics over domain-only grouping.
This is a coarse pass: mixed or large buckets are acceptable because a second pass will refine them.
Every eligible tab id must appear exactly once, either in buckets[].tabIds or reviewTabIds.
Put generic, sensitive, or very uncertain tabs in reviewTabIds.
Use sequenceIndex and index as ordering signals. Adjacent tabs often belong together.
Do not create a broad catch-all bucket for unrelated leftovers; use reviewTabIds instead.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.

error.message: The free gateway is temporarily rate limited. Please try later or use a custom gateway.

error.name: Error
error.message: 默认 AI 服务这次没有成功完成。请稍后再试，或在更多选项里临时切换自定义 AI 网关。

results.scenarioLabel: Task bursts with natural tab order

settings.customPrompt: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.

This is a fast first-pass software engineering task for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one compact JSON object. Do not include markdown or prose outside JSON.
Shape: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"tabIds":[1],"reason":"Short reason."}],"reviewTabIds":[2]}
Use tabIds arrays, not full tab objects.
Use at most 24 broad semantic buckets.
Allowed colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Prefer broad, useful semantic topics over domain-only grouping.
This is a coarse pass: mixed or large buckets are acceptable because a second pass will refine them.
Every eligible tab id must appear exactly once, either in buckets[].tabIds or reviewTabIds.
Put generic, sensitive, or very uncertain tabs in reviewTabIds.
Use sequenceIndex and index as ordering signals. Adjacent tabs often belong together.
Do not create a broad catch-all bucket for unrelated leftovers; use reviewTabIds instead.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.

Software engineering task input: create broad semantic buckets for these browser tabs.
Return compact coarse-bucket JSON only.
{"settings":{"organizeMode":"consolidate_one_window","targetWindowMode":"current_window","existingGroupMode":"dissolve_existing_groups","reviewGroupMode":"create_review_group","urlPrivacyMode":"sanitized_url","languageMode":"auto","promptPreset":"conservative","minConfidenceToApply":0.65,"maxTabsPerGroup":40,"thinkingIntensity":"medium"},"scope":{"kind":"all_normal_windows","currentWindowId":null,"invocationWindowId":1,"windowIds":[1,2,3,4]},"windowFields":["id","type","focused","incognito","tabCount"],"windows":[[1,"normal",true,false,30],[2,"normal",false,false,30],[3,"normal",false,false,30],[4,"normal",false,false,30]],"tabFields":["id","windowId","index","sequenceIndex","title","hostname","sanitizedUrl","urlKind","audible","discarded","sampleable","existingGroup","pageSample"],"tabs":[[10000,1,0,0,"Agent workbench 000","github.com","https://github.com/ai-coding/codex/0","web",false,true,true,"",null],[10001,1,1,1,"Chrome extension APIs -
